stories.title: Kendall Wells Breaks NCAA Freshman HR Record With No. 31

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Kendall Wells just made history.

HR No. 31 in Game 2 against Texas — the NCAA all-time freshman single-season home run record is now HERS.

Three-run shot in the 5th clinched the Red River series for Oklahoma. 🥎

#BoomerSooner #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
31 home runs. As a freshman.

Kendall Wells owns the NCAA all-time freshman single-season HR record. She still needs 6 more to break the ALL-TIME record of 37.

The chase isn't over. 🔥

#BoomerSooner #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 9:30 AM ET*

History was made Saturday night in Austin.

Kendall Wells hit home run No. 31 in the fifth inning of Oklahoma's 4-3 win over Texas — and with that swing, she officially became the greatest freshman power hitter in NCAA softball history. The three-run shot broke the all-time freshman single-season home run record of 30, previously shared by Lauren Chamberlain, Janie Reed Espinoza, and Dejah Mulipola.

Think about the stage: a back-and-forth Game 2 of the Red River Showdown, on the road against the defending national champions, with the SEC title and a postseason seed all hanging in the balance. Wells didn't just break the record — she did it in the most dramatic situation imaginable.

And she's not done. Six more home runs would break the all-time NCAA single-season record of 37, set by Espinoza at Arizona back in 1995 — a record that has stood for 31 years. Wells is on a pace no freshman has ever approached. She has 32 regular-season games remaining.

This is one of those seasons you'll tell people you watched live.

What do you think — can Wells get to 37 and break the all-time record too?
facebook.image_caption:
*Post with action photo*

Kendall Wells just made history. HR No. 31 breaks the NCAA all-time freshman single-season home run record. Six more and she's got the ALL-TIME mark too. 🥎

article.headline: History Made: Kendall Wells Breaks NCAA Freshman Home Run Record With No. 31

stories.title: Oklahoma Claims Red River Showdown Series 2-0

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Oklahoma wins the Red River Showdown series.

Sooners took Game 1 3-0 (Lowry CG shutout) and Game 2 4-3 on Wells' record-setting three-run HR.

OU leads the SEC by 2 games. 🥎

#BoomerSooner #SECSoftball #NCAASoftball

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Texas' Teagan Kavan recorded her 500th career strikeout in Game 1 of this series.

And still lost twice.

Oklahoma is just built different this year. No. 2 in the country for a reason. 🥎

#BoomerSooner #HookEm #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 12:00 PM ET*

Oklahoma didn't just win the Red River Showdown — they came into Austin and took it.

Game 1 on Friday set the tone. Audrey Lowry threw a complete-game shutout, holding the defending national champions scoreless. Ella Parker homered in the first, Isabela Emerling added a solo shot in the sixth, and just like that the Sooners were up 1-0 in the series. Texas pitcher Teagan Kavan recorded her 500th career strikeout in that game — a genuine milestone — and it still wasn't enough.

Then came Saturday. Oklahoma 4, Texas 3. Kendall Wells' record-breaking three-run homer in the fifth inning gave the Sooners the lead and clinched the series. McCombs Field, where Texas had gone 17-1 this season, couldn't stop what OU brought this weekend.

The SEC implications are real. Oklahoma now leads the conference by two games. A Texas sweep would have made this a race. Instead, the Sooners tighten their grip and head into the rest of the season with a statement series win behind them.

Game 3 is still on Sunday at 1 p.m. CT on ESPN — but the series belongs to Oklahoma.

#BoomerSooner
facebook.image_caption:
*Post with celebration photo*

Oklahoma. At McCombs. Series won. Audrey Lowry's shutout and Kendall Wells' record-breaking homer did the job. Sooners take the Red River Showdown 2-0.

stories.title: Stanford Sweeps FSU — Seminoles Fall in Two at Palo Alto

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Stanford just swept Florida State.

Stanford 9, FSU 8 on Friday (walk-off grand slam). Stanford 7, FSU 2 on Saturday.

The Seminoles drop two straight road games to a ranked team. 😳

#Noles #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
Addyson Sheppard's walk-off grand slam ended FSU's 25-game win streak on Friday.

Then Stanford won Game 2 by five.

FSU enters the Week 10 poll with back-to-back losses. Their season isn't over — but it just got harder. 🥎

#Noles #ACCSoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 1:30 PM ET*

Two days in Palo Alto, and Florida State leaves empty-handed.

Friday's walk-off grand slam by Addyson Sheppard — delivered in the bottom of the seventh after FSU had mounted a remarkable comeback — ended the Seminoles' 25-game winning streak. That was stunning enough. But Saturday, Stanford came back with a 7-2 win to finish the sweep and make sure there was no doubt about what just happened.

FSU now has back-to-back losses for the first time this season. Coming into the weekend, the Seminoles hadn't lost since February. Now they've dropped two road games to a ranked opponent, and the national conversation around Florida State has shifted significantly.

What does this mean for the rankings? FSU entered the weekend at No. 5 in the NFCA poll. They'll fall — possibly out of the top 10. The Week 10 poll comes out Tuesday, and the damage could be real.

What it doesn't mean is that FSU is no longer a tournament team. Isa Torres is still batting elite numbers, the staff is still talented, and one bad road trip doesn't define a postseason run. But the road to a top seed just got harder.

Were you watching this weekend? What did you make of the Stanford sweep?
facebook.image_caption:
*Post with game photo*

Stanford swept Florida State — back-to-back losses end FSU's remarkable run. The walk-off grand slam ended the streak Friday. The 7-2 loss Saturday sealed the sweep. Now the Seminoles face a real rankings hit. 😳

stories.title: Coach Patrick Murphy Becomes First SEC Coach to 500 Conference Wins

x_posts.label: Post 4-1
x_posts.text:
Alabama's Patrick Murphy became the first SEC softball coach to ever reach 500 conference wins.

500-205 (.709). No other SEC coach has even reached 400.

Alabama sweeps Auburn. Now 38-3. 🥎

#RollTide #SECSoftball #CollegeSoftball

x_posts.label: Post 4-2
x_posts.text:
Patrick Murphy's 500th SEC win came in a shutout.

Vic Moten threw a CG shutout, allowing 1 hit. Alabama clinched the series at Auburn.

The Crimson Tide also jumped to No. 1 in the Softball America rankings. 🔥

#RollTide #SECSoftball #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 3:30 PM ET*

It happened Saturday in Auburn, and it belongs in the history books.

Alabama head coach Patrick Murphy won his 500th conference game against Auburn — becoming the first softball coach in SEC history to reach that milestone. Not the first recently. The first ever. No other SEC coach has even reached 400 conference wins.

His record in the SEC now stands at 500-205, a winning percentage of .709. That's not just longevity — that's sustained excellence over decades of competition against the most talented conference in college softball.

The game itself was a fitting tribute. Pitcher Vic Moten threw a complete-game shutout against Auburn, allowing just one hit in a 4-0 Alabama win. The Crimson Tide clinched the series at Auburn and did so the way great Alabama teams do — with clean, dominant softball.

And the context makes it even richer: Alabama swept the series, improved to 38-3 on the season, and is now the No. 1 team in the Softball America rankings for the first time this season.

Murphy has been building something special in Tuscaloosa for a very long time. Saturday, it received the recognition it deserves.

#RollTide
facebook.image_caption:
*Post with coach photo*

Patrick Murphy: 500 SEC wins. First coach in conference history to reach that number. Alabama sweeps Auburn to get there. 🏆

stories.title: Nebraska Sweeps Wisconsin — 7 Straight Wins, 13-1 Big Ten

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Nebraska swept Wisconsin — blanked the Badgers twice (5-0 and 6-0).

Seven straight wins. Now 32-6, 13-1 in the Big Ten.

The Huskers belong in every No. 1 national seed conversation. 👀

#Huskers #BigTen #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 5:30 PM ET*

Nebraska didn't just beat Wisconsin this weekend — they dominated them in two consecutive shutouts.

Friday: Nebraska 5, Wisconsin 0. Wisconsin managed just 2 hits all night.
Saturday: Nebraska 6, Wisconsin 0. All six runs came in a single inning.

The Huskers have now won seven straight games and own a 13-1 record in Big Ten play. At 32-6 overall, they've built one of the most impressive résumés in the country for a program that doesn't always get the national recognition it deserves.

For anyone building WCWS projections: Nebraska's run differential, Big Ten dominance, and pitching depth all point to a team capable of making a deep tournament run. The conversation about top-8 national seeds needs to include Lincoln.

Seven straight wins. Two shutouts in a weekend. The Big Ten title is Nebraska's to lose.

What do you think — does Nebraska get a top-8 national seed this year?
facebook.image_caption:
*Post with team photo*

Nebraska swept Wisconsin — back-to-back shutouts (5-0 and 6-0). Seven straight wins. 13-1 in the Big Ten. The Huskers are making a national seed case nobody can ignore. 👀

stories.title: Texas Tech Falls to Utah in Game 2 — Series Tied 1-1

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
UPSET ALERT: Utah beats No. 1 Texas Tech 4-0 on Saturday.

Two weather delays (nearly 5 hours). The Red Raiders held scoreless.

Series tied 1-1 heading into Sunday's decider. 🥎

#WreckEm #CollegeSoftball #NCAASoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 4:00 PM ET*

Nobody beats No. 1 Texas Tech easily.

And yet, Utah did exactly that on Saturday — a 4-0 shutout of the nation's top-ranked team in a game stretched to nearly five hours by two separate weather delays. Game 1 went to Texas Tech 10-2. Game 2 went to Utah 4-0. Sunday's Game 3 is now a winner-take-all decider.

This is the kind of road test that defines seasons. Texas Tech has rolled through every opponent this year, but road play against motivated teams in unusual conditions — delays, cold, a hostile environment — tests different things. Saturday tested the Red Raiders and found a crack.

For Utah, this is a genuine program moment. Beating the nation's No. 1 team in any fashion is a statement.

Game 3 is noon CT Sunday on ESPN+. NiJaree Canady will almost certainly be in the circle. The Red Raiders need to bounce back quickly.

What happens Sunday — does Texas Tech take the series, or does Utah complete the upset?
facebook.image_caption:
*Post with game photo*

Utah 4, Texas Tech 0. The nation's No. 1 team shut out on the road. Two weather delays, nearly five hours of softball, and an upset to talk about. Series tied 1-1, Game 3 is Sunday.

stories.title: Softball America Week 10 Rankings — Alabama Climbs to No. 1

x_posts.label: Post 7
x_posts.text:
Softball America Week 10 top five:

No. 1 Alabama
No. 2 Texas Tech
No. 3 Oklahoma
No. 4 Texas
No. 5 Nebraska

Alabama sits at the top for the first time this season. 🥎

#SECSoftball #NCAASoftball #CollegeSoftball

facebook.long_form:
*Posting Window: 8:00 PM ET*

Alabama is No. 1 for the first time this season.

Softball America's Week 10 composite rankings reflect a weekend of upheaval at the top of college softball. Alabama jumped to No. 1 after sweeping Auburn and completing a dominant week that included coach Murphy reaching 500 SEC wins. Texas Tech fell to No. 2 after dropping Game 2 to Utah. Oklahoma holds at No. 3, Texas at No. 4, Nebraska at No. 5.

The official NFCA Week 10 coaches' poll comes out Monday and will likely tell a similar story — though FSU's sweep by Stanford and Texas Tech's stumble at Utah may cause larger movement across the full rankings.

Here's the bigger picture: We're entering the final month of the regular season, and teams are separating. Alabama, Texas Tech, and Oklahoma are all contenders. Nebraska is right there. And Stanford — having just swept FSU — made a statement about their own tournament intentions.

The road to Oklahoma City is running through a very crowded top 5 right now.

Who do you think finishes as the No. 1 overall seed for the WCWS?
facebook.image_caption:
*Post with rankings graphic*

Softball America Week 10 is out. Alabama is No. 1 for the first time this season. Texas Tech No. 2, Oklahoma No. 3, Texas No. 4, Nebraska No. 5. The race to Oklahoma City is officially on. 🥎
